───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• I pushed the next release-facing docs cleanup through.

  Changed:

  - added a concise troubleshooting section to README.md
  - tightened root repo-surface notes in README.md so packages/embedder, packages/mobile, and the top-level exploration folders are
    explicitly framed as non-core
  - added packages/embedder/README.md to mark the Python embedder as transitional
  - added packages/mobile/README.md to mark the Android client as experimental
  - updated meta/preparing-for-release.md to mark those checklist items done

  I also cleaned the aim graph:

  - marked Verify clean-clone dev/start flows end-to-end and Add preferred-port fallback and resolved-port reporting as human-
    dependency, since both now depend on real-machine output from you
  - removed the accidental duplicate optional-packaging unclear aim
  - added your future voice direction as unclear:
    Evaluate telephone-style voice API for call-like interaction (4e70b439-0730-448f-a6bc-ac838a1eed59)

  No code tests were needed here because this pass was documentation and aim-graph cleanup only.

  The main remaining release blockers are still:

  - real-machine validation of npm install / npm run dev / npm run start
  - real-machine validation of node scripts/validate-local-runtime.mjs
  - the embedder replacement itself in packages/backend/src/embeddings.ts

  If you want, I’ll take the embedder replacement next and work toward removing the Python service from the default architecture
  rather than waiting on validator output.
